title: Recession Indicators Dashboard 2026
description: Track 8 key leading recession indicators including the Yield Curve and Sahm Rule to gauge the probability of a 2026 economic downturn.

mainEntity.name: What are the most reliable recession indicators for 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most reliable leading indicators include the 10-Year/2-Year Yield Curve inversion, the Sahm Rule (unemployment rate momentum), and the Leading Economic Index (LEI).
While no single indicator guarantees a recession, tracking multiple metrics like consumer confidence, credit spreads, and yield curves provides a probabilistic assessment of economic downturns.

mainEntity.name: What is the yield curve inversion?

acceptedAnswer.text: A yield curve inversion occurs when short-term interest rates (like the 2-Year Treasury) rise above long-term rates (like the 10-Year Treasury). Historically, this has preceded every major US recession.

mainEntity.name: How does the Sahm rule indicator work?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Sahm Rule signals the start of a recession when the three-month moving average of the national unemployment rate rises by 0.50 percentage points or more relative to its minimum during the previous 12 months.

mainEntity.name: What is the current recession probability for 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Recession probability models rely on the aggregate data of these 8 leading indicators. When multiple metrics breach their historical trigger thresholds simultaneously, the probability of a near-term recession increases significantly.
